allies.rationale_text: Active-listening helps constructive-feedback-models land because the receiver is more likely to hear correction when they feel accurately understood.
Auditability-traceability helps constructive-feedback-models stay specific by tying observations to documented behavior, standards, and consequences.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias makes constructive-feedback-models weaker by turning corrective evidence into something each side explains away instead of learning from.
antagonists.source_quote: demands openness to evidence that disconfirms one's current belief

structured_tensions.tension_text: Constructive-feedback-models conflicts with authority-bias when feedback delivery is shaped by the giver's rank rather than the quality of the observation, causing recipients to comply rather than learn.
structured_tensions.source_quote: constructive-feedback-models conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when feedback delivery is shaped by the giver's rank rather than the quality of the observation, causing recipients to comply rather than learn.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Constructive-feedback-models conflicts with confirmation-bias when feedback is selectively delivered or received to confirm existing beliefs about performance rather than surface genuine deviation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: constructive-feedback-models conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when feedback is selectively delivered or received to confirm existing beliefs about performance rather than surface genuine deviation.

curation_notes.summary: The relation surface stays on evidence-bearing, relationship-safe allies with confirmation-bias as the clearest bounded antagonist.
donor_drops: Did not widen into broad coaching ecosystems because the raw source is stronger on specific feedback mechanics and anti-defensiveness discipline.
open_questions: Metacognition is also a source-real ally, but active-listening and auditability-traceability were the more operational anchors.
